textPlain: Related: Jaguar Land Rover hack cost UK economy an estimated $2.5 billion, report says: https://news.ycombinator.com/item?id=45668008The 'tech' for both these is by guess who? TCS!
The fact that they put their AWS secret keys on their website is incredible.
So the author got nothing but a thank you out of it? That's a shame.
Security for most Indian companies - even conglomerates is a joke.Look at the websites - most look like they've not been upgraded since the 90s, with endless popups
Sending it with AES encryption(with the key that the client has access to) makes it even worse, as someone knew this shouldn't be shared to client yet they shared it anyway.
If you’ve ever worked with Indian outsourcing firms it’s not
At least there was a "thank you".Some go on to sue such researchers.

What are you using to browse?
